Class RewardState

java.lang.Object
View.Client.States.ConnectedState
View.Client.States.Connected.LoggedInState
View.Client.States.Connected.LoggedIn.GameSelectedState
View.Client.States.Connected.LoggedIn.GameSelected.PlayingState
View.Client.States.Connected.LoggedIn.GameSelected.Playing.RewardState
All Implemented Interfaces:
ClientState
public class RewardState extends PlayingState
Represents the state of the game where players can view their rewards. This state extends the PlayingState and allows players to visualize their rewards.

  • Constructor Details

    • RewardState

      public RewardState(Network network, String username, Game game)
      Constructs a RewardState with the specified network, username, and game. This constructor initializes the state and sets the view to ViewRewardsState.
      Parameters:
      network - The network connection for the game.
      username - The username of the player.
      game - The current game instance.

  • Method Details

    • getAvailableCommands

      public List<String> getAvailableCommands()
      Returns the name of the state. This method is used for debugging and logging purposes.
      Specified by:
      getAvailableCommands in interface ClientState
      Overrides:
      getAvailableCommands in class PlayingState
      Returns:
      A string representing the name of the state.

    • viewRewards

      public void viewRewards()
      Executes the action to view rewards. This method is called when the player chooses to view their rewards.
